client_context.facility_line: Hamburg Bakery & Rietberg Cold Cuts, Federal Republic of Germany
client_context.jurisdiction_label: EU & German Sustainability Frameworks for Food Manufacturers

executive_summary:
February 2026 marks a pivotal compliance inflection point for Gerold Foods' Hamburg and Rietberg operations. Three frameworks with direct operational impact reached critical milestones this period: the German Packaging Act (VerpackG) fee schedule for 2026 was published by LUCID, the first CSRD sustainability report is due for fiscal year 2025 by 30 June 2026, and the Lieferkettensorgfaltspflichtengesetz (LkSG) high-risk supplier list was expanded to cover 47 additional tier-2 categories including meat processing intermediaries relevant to the Rietberg facility.

The EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation (PPWR, Regulation (EU) 2025/40) entered into force on 11 February 2025 and will apply from February 2030 for most requirements. Recycled content mandates for plastic contact packaging—affecting trays, films, and PE liners used in the Hamburg Bakery—are the highest-cost compliance vector identified this cycle. Minimum recycled content of 10% for non-bottle rigid plastic packaging is required by 2030, rising to 25% by 2040. Current supplier contracts do not specify recycled-content percentages; this gap must be closed at the next procurement review.

LkSG annual reporting obligations, now covering suppliers with 1,000+ employees, require Gerold Foods to submit its second annual report by 30 April 2026 to the Bundesamt für Wirtschaft und Ausfuhrkontrolle (BAFA). The 2025 report revealed two tier-1 suppliers in the Rietberg cold-cuts supply chain with unresolved corrective action plans from 2024 audits. These must be remediated or escalated to BAFA before the April deadline to avoid administrative penalties of up to EUR 800,000.

sections.heading: VerpackG — 2026 Licence Fee Schedule & LUCID Registration
paragraphs: The Zentrale Stelle Verpackungsregister (ZSVR) published the updated LUCID fee schedule for calendar year 2026 on 15 January 2026. Gerold Foods' Hamburg Bakery falls under categories B3 (bakery product packaging, paper/cardboard) and C1 (plastic primary packaging for food). The 2026 base participation fee for category C1 increased by 12.4% compared to 2025 following the expanded definition of service packaging included by the Zweites Gesetz zur Änderung des Verpackungsgesetzes (VerpackGÄndG 2024).

The Rietberg Cold Cuts facility must update its LUCID registration by 31 March 2026 to reflect the introduction of modified atmosphere packaging (MAP) trays under the new MAP sub-category introduced in November 2025. Failure to register MAP units separately will result in under-reporting of plastic tonnage, which triggers a corrective assessment under § 33 VerpackG with a fine of up to EUR 100,000.

Additionally, the dual obligation under the German Commercial Code (HGB) § 315b requires that packaging cost disclosures appear in the Nachhaltigkeitsbericht if they exceed the materiality threshold of 1% of cost of goods sold. The Compliance & Reporting team should confirm whether the 12.4% fee increase pushes Gerold Foods above this threshold for the 2025 fiscal year report.
sections.callout: Action required: Update Rietberg LUCID registration for MAP trays by 31 March 2026. Confirm HGB § 315b materiality threshold for packaging costs.

sections.heading: CSRD / ESRS — First Sustainability Report Due June 2026
paragraphs: Gerold Foods GmbH, with consolidated annual turnover exceeding EUR 250 million, is a large undertaking subject to the Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD, Directive 2022/34/EU as transposed by the CSR-Richtlinie-Umsetzungsgesetz into German law as § 289b–289e HGB). The first CSRD-compliant sustainability report, covering fiscal year 2025, must be filed with the Bundesanzeiger (Federal Gazette) by 30 June 2026 and audited by a Wirtschaftsprüfer to limited assurance standard.

The European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS) mandatory for Gerold Foods include: ESRS E1 (Climate Change — Scope 1, 2, and material Scope 3 categories), ESRS E5 (Resource Use and Circular Economy, directly relevant to packaging), ESRS S1 (Own Workforce), and ESRS G1 (Business Conduct, including LkSG alignment). Double materiality assessments completed in 2025-Q3 identified packaging circularity and supply chain human rights as material impact topics.

The EFRAG Q&A platform issued two clarifications in February 2026 relevant to food manufacturers: (1) own-farm emissions from direct agricultural inputs must be disclosed under ESRS E1 AR-6 if the reporting entity exercises operational control, and (2) the 'value chain' disclosure threshold under ESRS E5 for packaging waste applies even to packaging where the manufacturer bears EPR responsibility indirectly through a compliance scheme such as LUCID.
sections.callout: Deadline: CSRD sustainability report due 30 June 2026. Appoint Wirtschaftsprüfer for limited assurance immediately if not already done.

sections.heading: LkSG — Supply Chain Due Diligence: 2026 Annual Report & BAFA Enforcement
paragraphs: The Lieferkettensorgfaltspflichtengesetz (LkSG, BGBl. I 2021 S. 2959) requires Gerold Foods to submit its annual due diligence report to BAFA by 30 April 2026 via the BAFA online portal. The 2025 submission disclosed two tier-1 suppliers in the Rietberg cold cuts supply chain — a Polish pork primal cuts processor and a Dutch animal feed additive supplier — with open corrective action plans (CAPs) following 2024 on-site audits. As of February 2026, neither supplier has provided evidence of CAP completion.

BAFA published updated enforcement guidelines in January 2026 (Prüfleitfaden LkSG v2.1) that expand the definition of 'concrete indication' of human rights risk to include audit non-conformances unresolved for more than 12 months. Both open CAPs now qualify as concrete indications, triggering a mandatory risk mitigation measure under § 7 LkSG. Gerold Foods must either (a) obtain and submit completed CAP evidence, (b) request a BAFA-approved remediation extension, or (c) suspend the supplier relationship and source alternatively before the April deadline.

The Federal Government's 2026 LkSG impact assessment, published 3 February 2026, flags the food processing sector as high-risk for forced labour in upstream agricultural supply chains. BAFA has indicated sector-specific spot audits will commence in Q3 2026. Proactive engagement with BAFA before the April report reduces the probability of being selected for a spot audit.
sections.callout: Critical: Two open LkSG corrective action plans must be resolved before 30 April 2026 BAFA submission. Engage suppliers immediately and obtain signed CAP closure certificates.

sections.heading: EU PPWR — Recycled Content Mandates for Plastic Packaging (2030/2040)
paragraphs: Regulation (EU) 2025/40 on packaging and packaging waste (PPWR) entered into force on 11 February 2025. Article 7 establishes minimum recycled content requirements for plastic packaging placed on the EU market. For non-bottle rigid plastic packaging in contact with food — the category covering the Hamburg Bakery's PE trays and Rietberg's MAP containers — the mandatory minimum is 10% post-consumer recycled (PCR) content by 1 January 2030 and 25% PCR by 1 January 2040.

The European Commission's implementing regulation specifying measurement and verification methodology for PCR content is expected by Q4 2026. Until that regulation is published, manufacturers are advised to document supplier declarations using the CEN/TS 17657 framework as interim evidence. Gerold Foods' current packaging supplier contracts contain no recycled-content specification clauses, representing a compliance gap that must be addressed at the next contract renewal cycle, the earliest of which is October 2026.

Article 26 of PPWR establishes reuse targets for group-4 transport packaging (reusable crates and pallets) that apply immediately from 2025. Gerold Foods' outbound logistics to retail partners use single-use corrugated trays. A transition plan to a pooled reusable crate system (e.g., CHEP or equivalent) should be initiated no later than 2027 to demonstrate regulatory trajectory alignment and avoid non-conformance notices from retail partners subject to their own PPWR reporting obligations.
sections.callout: Action required: Insert PCR content specification clauses into packaging supplier contracts at next renewal (October 2026). Commission baseline PCR content audit from packaging supplier by Q3 2026.

sections.heading: EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) — Cocoa & Soy Supply Chain Compliance
paragraphs: Regulation (EU) 2023/1115 on deforestation-free supply chains (EUDR) applies to operators placing cocoa, soy, cattle, wood, coffee, palm oil, and derived products on the EU market. Gerold Foods' Hamburg Bakery uses cocoa powder and soy lecithin as ingredients, making it an 'operator' under Article 2(15) EUDR. The deferred enforcement date, following the December 2024 amendment, is 30 December 2025 for large operators — Gerold Foods is already in the compliance window.

Due diligence statements (DDS) must be submitted via the EU Information System (EUDR IS) before placing products containing regulated commodities on the market. As of February 2026, the EUDR IS is operational for cocoa and soy. Gerold Foods must obtain geolocation data (polygon or point coordinates) from its cocoa and soy suppliers covering the plots of land where the commodities were produced. At least two Hamburg Bakery suppliers have not yet responded to the geolocation data request sent in December 2025.

The German Federal Agency for Agriculture and Food (BLE) is the competent authority for EUDR enforcement in Germany. BLE published its national inspection plan on 20 January 2026, targeting food manufacturers in the Hamburg metropolitan area in Q2 2026. Gerold Foods should prioritise obtaining outstanding geolocation data and completing DDS submissions for the highest-volume cocoa and soy suppliers before April 2026.
sections.callout: Urgent: Obtain outstanding EUDR geolocation data from 2 suppliers before Q2 2026 BLE inspection window. Submit due diligence statements to EUDR IS immediately upon data receipt.

rows: Update MAP tray registration; verify HGB § 315b threshold
File FY2025 sustainability report with limited assurance
Resolve two open CAPs; submit BAFA annual report
Insert PCR content clauses in packaging contracts
Obtain supplier geolocation data; submit DDS to EUDR IS

critical_actions.action: Obtain CAP closure certificates from Polish and Dutch tier-1 suppliers
Appoint Wirtschaftsprüfer for limited assurance of FY2025 sustainability report
Obtain geolocation polygon data from 2 outstanding cocoa/soy suppliers
Register MAP tray packaging category in LUCID before deadline
